body:
## Summary

When https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/pull/5314 introduced the possibility to use `PerturbationAdvection` tracers, it didn't actually change the calculation of the advection velocity.

This PR takes a closer look at that and fixes two independent problems.

### 1. Advecting velocity

The radiation phase speed was computed from the field's *own* exterior value:

```julia
c = ψ̄ + c★
```

For a boundary-normal velocity component, `ψ̄` *is* the advecting velocity, so this is correct. For a tracer, `ψ̄` is the tracer value (e.g. a temperature), and using it as an advection speed is physically meaningless — the inflow/outflow timescale selection (`ψ̄ >= 0`) was likewise testing the sign of the *tracer* rather than of the *flow*.

The scheme now takes the **boundary-normal flow velocity** as the phase speed. `step_right/left_open_boundary!` accept an explicit advecting velocity:

- velocity (`Face`) halo methods pass `nothing` and fall back to `ψ̄` → **momentum behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged**;
- tracer (`Center`) halo methods pass the normal velocity component (`u`/`v`/`w`) read from `model_fields` at the boundary face (no transverse interpolation needed — the normal velocity is already center-located in the transverse directions).

### 2. Vertical (top/bottom) boundaries

`_fill_top_halo!` / `_fill_bottom_halo!` only had methods for `Face`-located fields. A tracer with a `PerturbationAdvection` BC on `top`/`bottom` silently fell through to the generic open-BC halo fill (a plain prescribed-value condition). Added the missing `Center` methods.

## Tests

Added `test_perturbation_advection_tracer_open_boundary_conditions` to `test/test_boundary_conditions_integration.jl`, run alongside the existing velocity test in the `Open boundary conditions` testset. It uses `HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel` (the model that fills tracer open boundaries):

- **Lateral (east/west):** a uniform flow makes one x-boundary inflow and the other outflow. Inflow relaxes instantly to the exterior value; outflow radiates and preserves the uniform interior. Same tracer config, opposite velocity → opposite results — the case that fails on the old code.
- **Vertical (top/bottom):** in a quiescent column the scheme freezes the boundary, so a uniform tracer stays uniform — confirming the new `Center` top/bottom methods run the radiation scheme instead of the plain value-BC fallback.

Verified locally (CPU): the new tracer test and the existing velocity regression test both pass (15/15 `@test`s). `test_quality_assurance.jl` left to CI.

## Scope / follow-ups

Deliberately **not** in this PR:

- **West/south/bottom `Center` index asymmetry** — the low-side `Center` halo methods write the first interior cell rather than the halo cell. Pre-existing; should be addressed separately.
- **`NonhydrostaticModel` plumbing** — tracer halos are filled there with `fill_open_bcs=false`, so tracer open BCs are currently inert in the nonhydrostatic model. `HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel` already fills them (`fill_open_bcs=true`). Enabling the nonhydrostatic path is a time-stepping change for a separate PR.
- **Density-weighted advecting velocity** — for anelastic models with prognostic `ρu`, the extracted `U` would be momentum, not velocity; converting it via `to_intensive` is left as follow-up.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Support tracers in `PerturbationAdvection` open boundary conditions

body:
I think before we had a regularization that was made based on the location (i.e. fields on edges had sign -1 while fields on nodes and centers 1), I think we lost it along the way. It might make sense to bring it back since also ClimaSeaIce is now wrong.

However, this is a quickfix in the meantime that we still require.
title: Regularize correctly `U` and `V` bc for a `TripolarGrid`
